data.content:
World Athletics Championship
This entity is not relevant to the input text. (Removed)<SEP>The World Athletics Championship is a global sports competition featuring top athletes in track and field.

Tokyo
Tokyo is the host city of the World Athletics Championship.

Noah Carter
Noah Carter is a sprinter who set a new record in the 100m sprint at the World Athletics Championship.

100m Sprint Record
The 100m sprint record is a benchmark in athletics, recently broken by Noah Carter.

Carbon-Fiber Spikes
Carbon-fiber spikes are advanced sprinting shoes that provide enhanced speed and traction.

World Athletics Federation
The World Athletics Federation is the governing body overseeing the World Athletics Championship and record validations.

Lipo-Hipertrofia
Lipo-hypertrophy is a condition where the skin becomes thickened and less sensitive to pain.

Glicemia
Blood sugar levels need to be controlled in people with diabetes.<SEP>Glycemia refers to the level of glucose in the blood.<SEP>Glicemia refers to the level of glucose in the blood.<SEP>Glicemia refers to blood sugar levels.

Diabetes Mellitus
Diabetes mellitus is a disease where the body cannot produce or use insulin correctly.<SEP>O diabetes mellitus é uma condição crônica que afeta a produção de insulina.<SEP>Diabetes mellitus is a chronic condition characterized by high blood sugar levels.

Pâncreas
The pancreas is an organ in the body that produces insulin.<SEP>The pancreas is an organ responsible for producing insulin and other hormones.

Sistema de Defesa
The immune system is a network of cells, tissues, and organs that defend the body against pathogens.

Diabetes Tipo 1
Type 1 diabetes is a condition where the body stops producing insulin due to an autoimmune response.

Diabetes Tipo 2
Type 2 diabetes is a condition where the body produces insulin, but it does not function correctly and production decreases over time.

Diabetes Mellitus Tipo 1 e 2
Diabetes mellitus type 1 and 2 are conditions where the body cannot produce or use insulin correctly.

Pâncreas Humano
The human pancreas is an organ responsible for producing insulin and other hormones.

Diabetes Mellitus Tipo 2 e Insulina
Type 2 diabetes is characterized by resistance to insulin and decreased production over time.

Canetas
Canetas are medical devices used for insulin administration.

Álcool 70%
Álcool 70% is a type of disinfectant used to clean equipment.<SEP>Álcool 70% is a disinfectant used to clean the caneta and skin before application.

Algodão
Algodão is a material used for cleaning the caneta.

Insulina Leitosa
Insulina Leitosa is a type of insulin used in medical treatment.

Insulina
Insulina, also known as insulin, is a hormone produced by the pancreas that plays a crucial role in regulating blood sugar levels. It is a medication used to treat diabetes, a condition characterized by high blood sugar levels due to inadequate insulin production or utilization. Insulin helps cells absorb glucose from the bloodstream, thereby reducing blood sugar levels.

Insulina is administered through various routes, including subcutaneous injections and intravenous infusions. The medication is typically prescribed for individuals with type 1 diabetes, who require exogenous insulin to control their blood sugar levels. In some cases, Insulina may also be used to treat type 2 diabetes, particularly in patients who have not responded adequately to oral medications.

As a medication, Insulina can cause hypoglycemia, a condition characterized by low blood sugar levels. It is essential for individuals taking Insulina to monitor their blood glucose levels regularly and adjust their dosage accordingly to avoid hypoglycemic episodes. Additionally, patients should be aware of the signs and symptoms of hypoglycemia, such as dizziness, sweating, and tremors.

In summary, Insulina is a hormone produced by the pancreas that regulates blood sugar levels. It is a medication used to treat diabetes, which can cause hypoglycemia if not managed properly. Individuals taking Insulina should be aware of their blood glucose levels and adjust their dosage accordingly to avoid complications. |

Agulha
An agulha is a fine and short needle used in canetas de insulina, available in sizes 4, 5, or 6 mm.<SEP>Agulha is a medical device used for insulin administration.<SEP>An agulha is a medical tool used for injecting insulin.<SEP>The agulha is the needle used to inject insulin into the skin.<SEP>A device used for injecting insulin into the body.

Unidade de Saúde
Unidade de Saúde is an organization responsible for healthcare services.

Glicose
The glicose is a type of sugar in the blood.<SEP>Glicose is a measure of blood sugar levels.<SEP>Glicose is a type of sugar that can be used as energy.

Hemoglobina Glicada (HbA1c)
HbA1c is a measure of average blood glucose levels over time.

Sensor de Glicose
O sensor de glicose é usado para monitorar a glicose continuamente.<SEP>O sensor de glicose é um pequeno dispositivo colado na pele que mede a glicemia o tempo todo.<SEP>The sensor de glicose is a device that measures blood glucose levels.<SEP>The Sensor of Glucose is a device that helps control diabetes.

Dexcom G6
Dexcom G6 is a type of continuous glucose monitoring system.

FreeStyle Libre
FreeStyle Libre is a type of continuous glucose monitoring system.

Medtronic MiniMed
Medtronic MiniMed is a type of insulin pump.

Glicosímetro
Um glicosímetro é um aparelho que mede a glicose no sangue.<SEP>A glicosímetro is a device used to measure blood sugar levels, but it requires pricking the finger.<SEP>Um glicosímetro é um dispositivo que mede a glicemia.<SEP>The glicosímetro is a device that measures blood glucose levels.<SEP>A Glicosímetro is a device used to measure blood glucose levels.<SEP>A glicosímetro is a device used to measure blood glucose levels.

Dose de Insulina
A dose de insulina é um tratamento para diabetes.

Médico
O médico pode passar um esquema chamado "basal-bolus", que combina insulina de ação lenta (basal) + insulina de ação rápida (bolus, antes das refeições).<SEP>O médico ajuda a entender como está o controle do paciente ao longo do tempo.<SEP>A doctor is a medical professional who helps patients manage their health conditions, including diabetes.

Tratamento
O tratamento é uma medida que ajuda a curar a doença do paciente.<SEP>O tratamento é uma medida que ajuda a curar a doença do paciente<SEP>O tratamento com insulina é personalizado e depende do paciente.<SEP>Treatment refers to the management and care of a patient's condition, in this case, diabetes.

data.entity_name: Insulina Várias Vezes ao Dia ou Bomba de Insulina
data.content:
Insulina Várias Vezes ao Dia ou Bomba de Insulina
Essa categoria de pacientes precisa medir a glicemia pelo menos 4 vezes por dia.

data.entity_name: Esquema Basal-Bolus (Insulina Lenta + Insulina Antes das Refeições)
data.content:
Esquema Basal-Bolus (Insulina Lenta + Insulina Antes das Refeições)
Pacientes com esse esquema precisam medir até 7 vezes ao dia.

data.entity_name: Insulina Uma Vez por Dia ou Só Comprimidos
data.content:
Insulina Uma Vez por Dia ou Só Comprimidos
Essa categoria de pacientes pode medir 2 ou 3 vezes ao dia.

Glicosímetro (Sensor de Glicose)
O glicosímetro é um dispositivo que mede a glicemia sem precisar furar o dedo.

Insulina Lenta
A insulina lenta é um tipo de insulina que é administrada por via subcutânea.

Insulina Antes das Refeições
A insulina antes das refeições é um tipo de insulina que é administrada por via intravenosa.

HbA1c
HbA1c is a blood test that measures average blood sugar levels over the past 2-3 months.<SEP>HbA1c is a measure of average blood glucose control over time.

Tempo no Alvo (TIR)
Tempo no Alvo (TIR) measures the time spent within the ideal blood glucose range, between 70 and 180 mg/dL.

Sensores de Glicose
Sensores de Glicose are devices used to measure blood glucose levels.<SEP>Sensores de glicose are devices that measure blood glucose levels.

DM tipo 1
DM tipo 1 is a type of diabetes.

Crianças e adolescentes
Crianças e adolescentes refers to children and adolescents with DM tipo 1.

Adultos com diabetes tipo 1 ou 2
Adultos com diabetes tipo 1 ou 2 refers to adults with type 1 or type 2 diabetes.

Idosos (a partir de 65 anos)
Idosos (a partir de 65 anos) refers to seniors aged 65 and above.

INSULINOTERAPIA
Insulinoterapia is the treatment of diabetes with insulin.

Tratamento com Insulina
The treatment with insulin is a medical intervention for people with diabetes.

Diabetes tipo 1
Type 1 diabetes is a condition where the body does not produce insulin.

Diabetes tipo 2
Type 2 diabetes is a condition where the body produces insulin but it is not used effectively.

Açúcar no sangue
Blood sugar levels need to be controlled in people with diabetes.

Remédios orais
Oral medications are used to treat type 2 diabetes.

Comprimidos
Tablets are a form of oral medication for treating type 2 diabetes.

Gestação
Pregnancy is a condition where insulin therapy may be necessary.<SEP>Gestação is a period of pregnancy and childbirth.

Insulinaoterapia
Insulinoterapia is the treatment of diabetes with insulin.

Associação Americana de Diabetes
The American Diabetes Association is an organization that provides recommendations for diabetes management.

Local de Aplicação
The application site refers to the area on the body where insulin is administered.

Pelo
Skin is the natural barrier that covers the body and is affected by insulin administration.

Músculo
Muscle refers to the tissue beneath the skin where insulin can be absorbed.<SEP>Muscle tissue where insulin can be injected in emergency situations.

Gordura
Fat refers to the layer of tissue beneath the skin where insulin is absorbed.<SEP>Body fat where insulin is typically injected.<SEP>Gordura refers to fat, which can be burned by the body as an alternative source of energy.

Rodízio
Rotation refers to the practice of changing the application site for insulin administration.

Toalha Limpa
A toalha limpa é necessária para evitar a contaminação do teste.

Lanceta
Uma lanceta (agulhinha) é usada para furar o dedo.

Dedo
O dedo é a área onde se faz a punção.

Punção
A punção é o ato de furar o dedo para coletar sangue.

Sangue
O sangue é coletado durante a punção.

Fita
A fita é usada para coletar o sangue.

Diabetes
O diabetes é uma doença que afeta a produção de insulina no corpo humano.<SEP>Diabetes is a condition where the body cannot produce enough insulin, leading to high blood sugar levels.

Hipoglicemia
A hipoglicemia é uma condição em que a glicemia está muito baixa.<SEP>Hypoglycemia is a condition where the blood glucose level is too low.<SEP>Hipoglicemia refers to low blood sugar levels, typically below 70 mg/dL.<SEP>Hipoglicemia is a condition where the blood sugar level becomes too low.

Hiperglicemia
Hyperglycemia is a condition where the blood glucose level is too high.

Geladeira
The Insulina should be stored in the refrigerator between 2°C and 8°C, but not in the freezer.<SEP>The refrigerator is used to store insulin and other medications.<SEP>The Geladeira is the refrigerator where the insulina should be stored.<SEP>A geladeira é o local onde a insulina deve ser guardada para manter sua eficácia.

Educação em Diabetes
Educação em Diabetes refers to education about diabetes, including how to use insulin correctly.

Médico Elliott P. Joslin
Médico Elliott P. Joslin was a doctor who helped train nurses to teach people with diabetes how to care for themselves.

Enfermeiras
Enfermeiras are nurses who can help educate people about diabetes and how to use insulin correctly.

Paciente
The Paciente is the person with diabetes who needs to learn how to use insulin correctly.<SEP>O paciente é o indivíduo que precisa cuidar da sua saúde e do seu tratamento para diabetes.

Família
The Família refers to the family members and caregivers of a person with diabetes.

Prega
Prega refers to a subcutaneous injection technique used for insulin application.

Crianças Pequenas
Crianças pequenas are children who require specific considerations for insulin application due to their age and body composition.

Adultos e Crianças
Adults and children are both eligible for insulin application, but with different considerations based on age and body composition.

Agulhas Curtas
Agulhas curtas refers to short needles used for insulin application.

Pessoas Magras
Pessoas magras refers to individuals with a low body mass index who require specific considerations for insulin application.

barriga
The location where the insulin will be applied is referred to as "barriga".

pele
The skin or pele is a crucial area for the application of insulin.

álcool
Alcohol, specifically álcool 70%, is recommended to clean the skin before applying insulin in certain locations such as postos de saúde, escolas, or lares de idosos.

insulina
Insulin is a medication used to regulate blood sugar levels and is applied through injection into the skin.

caroços
Caroços, or lipodistrofia, are areas of hardened fat that should be avoided when applying insulin.

lipodistrofia
Lipodistrofia is a condition characterized by the hardening of fat tissue and should be avoided when applying insulin.

edema
Edema, or swelling, should also be avoided when applying insulin.

feridas
Feridas, or open wounds, should be avoided when applying insulin.

fístulas
Fístulas, or abnormal passages between organs or the skin, should also be avoided when applying insulin.

álcool 70%
Alcohol, specifically álcool 70%, is recommended to clean the skin before applying insulin.

data.entity_name: Agulhas maiores que 8 mm devem ser aplicadas sempre em 45° e não são recomendadas para crianças.
data.content:
Agulhas maiores que 8 mm devem ser aplicadas sempre em 45° e não são recomendadas para crianças.
This is a warning about the use of agulhas for children.

Seringa com agulha fixa
This is a recommendation for using a specific type of syringe.

Seringa do tamanho certo
This is a recommendation for using the correct size of syringe.

Local da aplicação com álcool 70%
This is an instruction for preparing the injection site.

Caneta de insulina
This is an instruction for using the insulin pen.

Agulha usada
This is an instruction for disposing of used syringe.

Mãe
Mãe is the mother during gestation.

Bebê
Bebê is the baby during gestation.

Jejum
Jejum is a period of fasting or not eating.

Café
Café is a type of beverage consumed during gestation.

Almoço
Almoço is a meal eaten during the day.

Jantar
Jantar is an evening meal eaten during gestation.

ACOG (EUA)
ACOG (EUA) is the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists.

ADA (EUA)
ADA (EUA) is the American Diabetes Association.

CDA (Canadá)
CDA (Canadá) is the Canadian Diabetes Association.

IDF (Internacional)
IDF (Internacional) is the International Diabetes Federation.

NICE (Reino Unido)
NICE (Reino Unido) is the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence.

pessoas
Pessoas are individuals who can have diabetes and use various methods to monitor their blood sugar levels.

1925
The year 1925 marks the beginning of using urine tests at home to detect sugar in urine.

xixi
Xixi is a colloquial term for urine, which was used in early methods to test for sugar levels.

1964
The year 1964 saw the introduction of the first blood glucose meter that measured sugar levels from a single drop of blood.

AMGC
AMGC (Automonitoramento da Glicemia Capilar) is a method of monitoring blood glucose levels using small punctures on the finger and a glucometer.

Monitoramento contínuo de glicose (SMCG)
SMCG (Sensor Monitor Contínuo de Glicose) is a method of continuously monitoring blood glucose levels using sensors attached to the skin.

Sensor flash de glicose
The Sensor "flash" de glicose is a type of sensor that measures blood glucose levels and requires a reader to display the results.

data.entity_name: Medidores que funcionam como um relógio ou uma lente de contato
data.content:
Medidores que funcionam como um relógio ou uma lente de contato
These are new types of sensors being developed that function like a watch or contact lens to measure blood glucose levels.

data.entity_name: Medidores que usam luz ou calor, sem precisar furar o dedo
data.content:
Medidores que usam luz ou calor, sem precisar furar o dedo
These are new types of sensors being developed that use light or heat without needing to puncture the skin.

data.entity_name: Sensores colocados como uma tatuagem, que mostram a glicose pela pele
data.content:
Sensores colocados como uma tatuagem, que mostram a glicose pela pele
These are new types of sensors being developed that can be attached like tattoos and show blood glucose levels through the skin.

SMCG
SMCG (Sensor Monitor Contínuo de Glicose) is a method of continuously monitoring blood glucose levels using sensors attached to the skin.<SEP>SMCG stands for Sistema de Monitoramento Contínuo de Glicose, which is a system that continuously monitors glucose levels.

Caneta
A device used for injecting insulin, specifically a pen-like injector.

Pele
The pele is the surface where the insulin is injected.<SEP>Skin where the needle is inserted to inject insulin.

Hospital
A medical facility where emergency injections of insulin may be administered.<SEP>O hospital é um local onde os pacientes podem ser atendidos e tratados por profissionais de saúde.

Veia
Blood vessels through which insulin can be injected in emergency situations.

Seringas
Seringas are medical devices used to administer insulin.

Agulha Removível
The Agulha Removível is a type of needle that should not be used with insulin syringes.<SEP>Agulha removível is a type of seringa that is not recommended for use with insulin due to its risks.

Crianças e Adolescentes
Children and adolescents often require small, odd doses of insulin, making smaller seringas ideal.

Profissionais de Saúde
Professionals in the healthcare industry are trained to use medical devices and medications safely.<SEP>Profissionais de saúde são responsáveis por cuidar dos pacientes com diabetes.

Equipamento de Segurança
Equipment for safety is necessary when using agulha removível.

Insulina NPH
Insulina NPH is a type of insulin that can be mixed with other types of insulin.<SEP>The Insulina NPH is a type of insulin that can be used in combination with Insulina Regular.

Meta de HbA1c
The Meta de HbA1c is a benchmark for blood glucose control.

Variabilidade Glicêmica
Variabilidade glicêmica refers to the fluctuation of blood glucose levels throughout the day.

Glucagon-Like Peptide-1 Receptor Agonist
Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists are medications used to treat type 2 diabetes.

Sulfonylurea
Sulfonylureas are medications used to treat type 2 diabetes by stimulating insulin release.

Metformin
Metformin is a medication used to treat type 2 diabetes by improving insulin sensitivity.

data.entity_name: Lifestyle Change Strategies and Techniques Development Programs Initiatives Initiatives
data.content:
Lifestyle Change Strategies and Techniques Development Programs Initiatives Initiatives
Lifestyle change strategies and techniques development programs initiatives initiatives is an essential part of managing diabetes mellitus.

Lipohipertrofia
Lipohipertrofia refers to the formation of caroços or lumps under the skin, which can be prevented by changing insulin injection sites.

Agulha Nova
A new needle is recommended for each application of insulin to avoid pain and inflammation.

Conselho de Saúde
A healthcare professional is recommended to consult for any concerns or questions about insulin use.<SEP>A healthcare professional is recommended for any concerns or questions about insulin use.

Seringa U100
The Seringa U100 is a type of syringe used to administer insulin, which should be used with the correct type of insulin.

Agulha Fixa
The Agulha Fixa is a type of needle that is recommended for use with insulin syringes.

Dose Pares
A dose pares refers to a dose of insulin that is measured in pairs.

Dose Ímpares
A dose ímpares refers to a dose of insulin that is measured in odd numbers.

Lugar Fresco
A place that is fresh and away from direct sunlight is recommended for storing insulin.

Dose Pequenas e Fracionadas
A dose pequenas e fracionadas refers to a small or fractional dose of insulin.

Conselso de Saúde
A healthcare professional is recommended for any concerns or questions about insulin use.

Insulina Regular
The Insulina Regular is a type of insulin that should be aspirated first when used in combination with NPH insulin.

NPH Insulin
The NPH insulin is another type of insulin that can be used in combination with Insulina Regular.

World Health Organization
The World Health Organization is the governing body overseeing health-related guidelines and recommendations.

Canetas de Insulina
Canetas de insulina are insulin pens that have become increasingly popular for their practicality and modern design.

Laboratórios
Laboratórios are the manufacturers of various types of insulin, including those that can only be used with caneta.

Caneta Descartável
The Caneta Descartável is a disposable insulin pen that comes pre-filled with insulin and is discarded when empty.

Caneta Recarregável
The Caneta Recarregável is a reusable insulin pen that uses cartridges (refis) containing 3 mL of insulin, which can be replaced when the cartridge runs out.

Refil
A refil is a cartridge containing 3 mL of insulin used in Caneta Recarregável.

Caneta de Insulina Recarregável
The Caneta de Insulina Recarregável is a reusable insulin pen that uses cartridges (refis) containing 3 mL of insulin, which can be replaced when the cartridge runs out.

Refil de Insulina
A refil de insulina is a cartridge containing 3 mL of insulin used in Caneta Recarregável.

Agulha de Insulina
An agulha de insulina is a fine and short needle used in canetas de insulina, available in sizes 4, 5, or 6 mm.

Prega Subcutânea
The prega subcutânea is a technique used to apply insulin.

Crianças
Children under 6 years old may require a prega subcutânea when using small needles.

Adolescentes e Adultos
Teenagers and adults with little body fat in the application area may need to use a prega subcutânea.

Gestantes
Pregnant women should consider using a prega subcutânea for safety reasons, even with short needles.

Bagagem de Mão
The Bagagem de Mão refers to the luggage that can be carried in one's hand, where insulin should be stored.

Luz Solar Direta
The Luz Solar Direta is direct sunlight which should not be exposed to insulin.

Porta-Luvas
The Porta-Luvas refers to the dashboard or console of a vehicle where insulin should not be left.

Bagageiro de Carro ou Ônibus
The Bagageiro de Carro ou Ônibus refers to the luggage compartment of a car or bus where insulin should not be left.

Frascos Lacrados
The Frascos Lacrados are sealed containers used for storing insulin, which must be kept between 2 and 8 degrees Celsius.

data.entity_name: Bolsas Térmicas com Gelox ou Gelo
data.content:
Bolsas Térmicas com Gelox ou Gelo
The Bolsas Térmicas com Gelox ou Gelo refers to insulated bags with gel packs used for storing insulin at the correct temperature.

Seringas e Agulhas Usadas
The Seringas e Agulhas Usadas are used syringes and needles which should be disposed of correctly.

data.entity_name: Lancetas (aquelas agulhinhas do teste de glicemia)
data.content:
Lancetas (aquelas agulhinhas do teste de glicemia)
The Lancetas refers to the lancets or small needles used for testing blood glucose levels, which should be disposed of correctly.

Canetas de Insulina Descartáveis
The Canetas de Insulina Descartáveis are disposable insulin pens which should be disposed of correctly.

Materiais da Bomba de Insulina
The Materiais da Bomba de Insulina refers to the materials used in an insulin pump, which should be disposed of correctly.

Coletor para Material Perfurocortante
The Coletor para Material Perfurocortante is a container specifically designed for disposing of sharp objects and materials contaminated with blood.

Unidade Básica de Saúde (UBS)
The Unidade Básica de Saúde (UBS) refers to the basic health unit where people can obtain free containers for disposing of used syringes and needles.

Insulina Rápida ou Ultrarrápida
Insulina rápida ou ultrarrápida é aplicada antes das refeições para controlar o açúcar que vem dos alimentos.

Insulina Intermediária, Lenta ou Ultralenta
Insulina intermediária, lenta ou ultralenta serve para manter a glicemia controlada ao longo do dia e da noite.

Caneta de Insulina
A insulina é sempre aplicada com uma agulha fina, mas existem formas diferentes de fazer isso: Caneta de insulina.<SEP>A caneta de insulina is a device used to inject insulin.

Seringa e Frasco
Método mais antigo, ainda usado em alguns casos.

Bomba de Insulina (SICI)
Um aparelhinho que fica na pele e aplica pequenas doses o dia todo.

Peso Corporal
A dose de insulina varia para cada pessoa e depende de: Peso corporal

Alimentação
A dose de insulina varia para cada pessoa e depende de: Alimentação (especialmente a quantidade de carboidratos)

Atividade Física
A dose de insulina varia para cada pessoa e depende de: Atividade física

Idade e Fase da Vida
A dose de insulina varia para cada pessoa e depende de: Idade e fase da vida (como a puberdade)

Situações Especiais
A dose de insulina varia para cada pessoa e depende de: Situações especiais (como febre ou infecção)

Monitorização Contínua de Glicose (CGM)
A monitorização contínua da glicose é uma tecnologia que mostra como está sua glicemia 24 horas por dia.

Intersticial
O intersticial é o líquido entre as células do corpo onde o açúcar presente é medido pelo sensor de glicose.

Açúcar
O açúcar presente no intersticial é medido pelo sensor de glicose.

Gestão da Diabetes
A gestão da diabetes é o tratamento contínuo para controlar a glicemia.

Bomba de Insulina
A bomba de insulina is a device that delivers insulin based on glucose level readings from the sensor.<SEP>Uma bomba de insulina é um dispositivo que administra insulina várias vezes ao dia.

Gestão Contínua de Glicose (CGM)
A gestão contínua da glicose é um tratamento que ajuda a regular a glicemia.

urina
Urinalysis is a method of testing for ketones in urine.

fitinha
A fitinha (test strip) is used to test for ketones in urine, similar to a pregnancy test.

sangue
Blood is the medium used to measure ketone levels using an apparatus similar to a glicosímetro.

β-hidroxibutirato (β-OHB)
β-hidroxibutirato (β-OHB) is a substance measured in blood to indicate ketosis.

glicosímetro
A glicosímetro is an apparatus used to measure glucose levels, similar to the one used for measuring β-OHB.

World Health Organization (WHO)
The World Health Organization (WHO) is not explicitly mentioned but is implied as a global health authority.

Sensor
A sensor is a small device attached to the skin that measures glucose levels 24 hours a day.

data.entity_name: Sistema de Monitoramento Contínuo de Glicose
data.content:
Sistema de Monitoramento Contínuo de Glicose
The Sistema de Monitoramento Contínuo de Glicose is a system that continuously monitors glucose levels without requiring frequent finger pricks.

Celular
A celular is a device used to display glucose levels measured by the sensor.

Receitas Médicas
As receitas médicas são documentos que contêm as instruções para o tratamento do paciente.

Abreviações em Receitas Médicas
As abreviações em receitas médicas podem causar confusões se forem mal interpretadas.

data.entity_name: Prateleiras do Meio ou de Baixo
data.content:
Prateleiras do Meio ou de Baixo
Os prateleiras do meio ou de baixo são os melhores lugares para guardar a insulina na geladeira.

Medicação
A medicação é um medicamento que ajuda a tratar a doença do paciente.

Enfermidade
A enfermidade é uma condição de saúde que afeta o corpo humano.

Glicose Baixa
Glicose baixa refers to low blood sugar levels.

Açúcar no Sangue
Açúcar no sangue is the level of glucose in the blood.

Tempo
Tempo refers to time or duration.

Carboidrato de Ação Rápida
Carboidrato de ação rápida refers to fast-acting carbohydrates.

Suco de Laranja
Suco de laranja is orange juice that can be used as a quick source of sugar.

Refrigerante Comum
Refrigerante comum refers to a common type of soda that contains sugar.

Açúcar Dissolvido em Água
Açúcar dissolvido em água is sugar dissolved in water.

Bala de Glicose
Bala de glicose refers to a type of candy that contains glucose.

Mel
Mel refers to honey, which can be used as a source of sugar.

Cetonas
Cetonas are substances produced by the body when it does not have enough glucose for energy and starts burning fat.

Diabetes Descompensado
Diabetes descompensado is a condition where diabetes becomes uncontrolled and blood sugar levels become too high.

Casos de Diabetes
Casos de diabetes refers to cases or instances of diabetes.

Cetoacidose Diabética
Cetoacidose diabética is a complication that can occur when cetonas accumulate in the body.

Braços
The braços are recommended locations for applying insulin.

Nádegas
The nádegas are recommended locations for applying insulin.

Coxas
The coxas are recommended locations for applying insulin.

Abdômen (barriga)
The abdômen is a recommended location for applying insulin during pregnancy.

Idosos muito comprometido
Avoiding high and low blood sugar levels is more important than focusing on numerical targets.

Variabilidade glicêmica
The lower the variation, the better.

Monitoramento Contínuo
O sensor de glicose é usado para monitorar a glicose continuamente.

Diabetes tipo 2 (DM2)
O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina, causando problemas nos olhos (retinopatia), dores e perda de sensibilidade nas mãos e pés (neuropatia) e infartos e derrames.<SEP>O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina, causando problemas nos olhos (retinopatia), dores e perda de sensibilidade nas mãas e pés (neuropatia) e infartos e derrames.<SEP>O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina, causando dores e perda de sensibilidade nas mãos e pés (neuropatia).<SEP>O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina, causando problemas nos olhos (retinopatia).<SEP>O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina, causando infartos e derrames.<SEP>O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina.

Sistema de defesa (imunidade)
O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina, causando problemas nos olhos (retinopatia), dores e perda de sensibilidade nas mãos e pés (neuropatia) e infartos e derrames.<SEP>O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina, causando problemas nos olhos (retinopatia), dores e perda de sensibilidade nas mãas e pés (neuropatia) e infartos e derrames.<SEP>O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina, causando dores e perda de sensibilidade nas mãos e pés (neuropatia).<SEP>O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina, causando problemas nos olhos (retinopatia).<SEP>O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina, causando infartos e derrames.<SEP>O sistema de defesa ataca as células do pâncreas que fazem insulina.

prega subcutânea
A prega subcutânea, or a "beliscão" made with fingers before applying insulin, helps to lift the layer of fat and avoid injecting into muscle.<SEP>Insulin is injected into the skin after making a prega subcutânea.

Mãe e Bebê
Gestação involves the mother and baby.

Almoço e Jantar
The mother eats almoço and jantar during gestation.

ACOG (EUA) e IDF (Internacional)
Glicemia levels should be managed according to ACOG and IDF guidelines.

Rodízio dos Locais de Aplicação da Insulina
The rodízio (rotation) of insulin application sites is important to avoid lipohipertrofia and ensure proper insulin absorption.
data.entity_name: Rodízio dos Locais de Aplicação da Insulina

data.content:
Dicas para Fazer o Rodízio da Forma Correta
The dicas (tips) for doing the rodízio correctly are guidelines to implement the rotation of insulin application sites.
data.entity_name: Dicas para Fazer o Rodízio da Forma Correta

data.content:
Lipodistrofia
Lipodistrofia is a change in the skin's tissue.

mudança no tecido da pele
Lipodistrofia is a change in the skin's tissue.

>glicosímetro
>Blood is measured using an apparatus similar to a glicosímetro.

Transporte
A insulina deve ser transportada de forma adequada para manter sua eficácia.

Pregas subcutâneas
Pregas subcutâneas are a medical technique used to apply the Insulina.
